title: Engineering and product hybrid background
content: Candidate has engineering plus product experience and can translate technical constraints into product requirements, roadmap tradeoffs, and cross-functional execution.

title: Monitoring Hub unified observability platform
content: Candidate drove a unified monitoring and observability platform for Microsoft Fabric workloads, focused on job discovery, failure diagnosis, cross-workload debugging, and reducing fragmented troubleshooting experiences.

title: High-adoption platform product growth
content: Candidate scaled a unified observability platform to 400K+ monthly active users, roughly 80% of Fabric MAU, by prioritizing core user scenarios over workload-specific feature parity.

title: Platform product execution in complex technical contexts
content: Candidate can connect user needs, technical constraints, roadmap tradeoffs, and launch planning in complex product or platform contexts.

title: Data workflow and execution platform experience
content: Candidate has experience with complex technical platforms, data workflows, job execution, monitoring, scheduling, execution identity, and operational reliability.

title: Enterprise execution scalability and throttling
content: Candidate worked on scheduling, concurrency, throttling, and long-running execution scenarios, including support for 1000+ executions per minute per user and enterprise migration constraints.

title: Enterprise migration and reliability constraints
content: Candidate identified and resolved execution constraints such as concurrency limits, API throttling, credential expiration, long-running job lifecycle issues, and reliability gaps in enterprise pipeline migration scenarios.

title: Monitoring and operational visibility product thinking
content: Candidate can reason about monitoring, operational visibility, failure awareness, and reliability-oriented product requirements for technical systems.

title: AI-driven monitoring and incident insight scenarios
content: Candidate explored AI-driven monitoring scenarios including failure summarization, pattern clustering, anomaly detection, notification, and alerting to reduce manual investigation effort.

title: AI-assisted customer feedback triage
content: Candidate used keyword filtering and semantic analysis to reduce noisy customer feedback review from roughly 100 posts per week to about 20 posts per week and improve response latency from days to hours.

title: AI-assisted PM, design, and engineering delivery pipeline
content: Candidate enabled a repeatable AI-assisted PM/design/engineering workflow from requirement synthesis to spec generation, design prototyping, and implementation support.

title: Measured impact from AI-assisted workflow adoption
content: Candidate reduced selected spec authoring and review cycles from about one week to about one day, and design iteration from weeks to days by applying AI to well-scoped workflow stages.

title: AI adoption boundaries and human judgment
content: Candidate can articulate where AI can fully automate well-scoped work and where human PM judgment remains necessary for scenario definition, prioritization, and outcome evaluation.

title: Cross-workload context-aware Copilot experience
content: Candidate contributed to thinking around cross-workload, context-aware AI assistant experiences with conversational workflows and persistent context.

title: SQL and data analysis for BI and product decisions
content: Candidate can use SQL and data analysis concepts to reason about BI, data intelligence, adoption metrics, and product performance.

title: Cross-functional product delivery
content: Candidate can work with engineering, design, business, and operations stakeholders to clarify requirements and drive product delivery.

title: English communication and global collaboration
content: Candidate has strong English communication and is suitable for global, cross-border, English-heavy, or documentation-heavy collaboration environments.

title: Frontend platform and UX engineering experience
content: Candidate built UI experiences and reusable components, with exposure to end-to-end testing, accessibility, unit testing, and user interaction quality.

title: DAG-like data job visualization experience
content: Candidate built a DAG-like visualization experience for streaming job execution relationships.

title: Backend data infrastructure and production support
content: Candidate worked on Hadoop infrastructure, logging systems, job queue management, open-source components, production support, and on-call ownership.

title: Data security and compliance product experience
content: Candidate worked on Hadoop-based data security and compliance products involving GDPR and sensitive data protection.

title: AI product and workflow evidence
content: Candidate has evidence around AI workflow, AI Agent, Data Agent, BI, AIGC, enterprise AI, productivity AI, and data intelligence through product exploration and the AI Job Search Copilot project.

title: Resume rewrite integrity rule
content: Resume tailoring must only reorder, compress, clarify, and re-narrate facts already present in the resume or supplied profile context. Missing JD requirements should become gaps or interview preparation, not fabricated resume claims.

title: Role-specific evidence selection
content: For each target JD, select the candidate evidence that best explains why the resume should emphasize certain experiences first. Prioritize proof of product execution, engineering fluency, data workflow, AI tooling, stakeholder collaboration, and measurable delivery when the JD asks for those signals.

title: AI application learning project
content: Candidate is building an AI job-search copilot using LLM structured extraction, deterministic scoring, prompt experiments, RAG-backed profile matching, and agent workflow concepts.

title: LLM structured output and schema validation learning
content: Candidate has practiced using LLM structured extraction, schema validation, and deterministic scoring to keep AI workflow outputs auditable.

title: RAG retrieval and evaluation learning
content: Candidate has practiced RAG-backed profile matching, top-k retrieval, evidence citations, and retrieval evaluation with recall@k.

title: Agent and function calling learning direction
content: Candidate is using this project to learn how fixed workflows can later evolve into tool calling and agent workflow orchestration.
